diagrams.title: Memory Hierarchy in UI Design
diagrams.description: Shows the three memory types and their characteristics relevant to interface design
diagrams.purpose: Understand memory constraints to design better interfaces

diagrams.title: Miller's Law Visualization
diagrams.description: Working memory capacity represented as containers holding chunks
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the 7±2 rule and why limiting options matters

diagrams.title: Serial Position Effect in Lists
diagrams.description: Shows how first and last items are remembered better than middle
elements: Middle items shown as easily forgotten
diagrams.purpose: Guide placement of important UI elements in lists and menus

diagrams.title: Ebbinghaus Forgetting Curve
diagrams.description: Graph showing memory retention over time without reinforcement
diagrams.purpose: Justify persistence of important information and repetition in UI
